title: Bracket for printed circuit board assembly
abstract: A bracket for a printed circuit board assembly (PCA) may comprise a first portion configured to be coupled to a first face of a first printed circuit board (PCB) and a second portion configured to be coupled to a first face of a second PCB. In an assembled state of the PCA, the first and second PCBs are in a stacked arrangement with the respective first faces thereof facing in a first direction. Moreover, in the assembled state, the bracket controls a distance between the respective first faces of the first and second PCBs along the first direction independent of respective thicknesses of the first and second PCBs along the first direction.
claims: 1. A bracket for a printed circuit board assembly (PCA), comprising: a first PCB connection portion configured to be coupled to a first face of a first printed circuit board (PCB) of the PCA in an assembled state of the PCA; and a second PCB connection portion configured to be coupled to a first face of a second PCB of the PCA in the assembled state of the PCA, wherein in the assembled state of the PCA, the first and second PCBs are in a stacked arrangement with the respective first faces thereof facing in a first direction; and the bracket controls a distance between the respective first faces of the first and second PCBs along the first direction independent of respective thicknesses of the first and second PCBs along the first direction.
